title: Is there a way to optimize this particular query?
description:
Hi all,
We have a working query for the following case, but it becomes very slow when querying our productive dataset.
Basically we want to have the triples returned where the subjects have <rdf:type> property defined somewhere, but we don't want only the triples returned where these type properties are defined. e.g., assuming the following data:
s1 - rdf:type - t1
s1 - p - o1
s2 - p - o2

So we want to have returned:
s1 - rdf:type - t1
s1 - p - o1

since s1 has type property while s2 doesn't.
Our SPARQL query which works on small datasets but not very well on large sets (for 7 million triples it took 1 hour) is the following:
SELECT * WHERE { ?s ?p ?o . { SELECT DISTINCT (?s) WHERE { ?s a [] } } }
So my question is if this query can be optimized in any way?
Cheers!
key_bug_features: Slow query performance on large SPARQL datasets
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE triples (subject VARCHAR(255), predicate VARCHAR(255), object VARCHAR(255), PRIMARY KEY (subject, predicate));
INSERT INTO triples (subject, predicate, object) VALUES ('s1', 'rdf:type', 't1');
INSERT INTO triples (subject, predicate, object) VALUES ('s1', 'p', 'o1');
INSERT INTO triples (subject, predicate, object) VALUES ('s2', 'p', 'o2');
SELECT * FROM triples WHERE subject IN (SELECT DISTINCT subject FROM triples WHERE predicate = 'rdf:type');
